person.name: Alice FitzAlan
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Alice FitzAlan was born circa 1289 to Richard FitzAlan, 8th Earl of Arundel (1266-1301) and Alasia di Saluzzo (-1292) and died 7 February 1340 of unspecified causes. She married Stephen de Segrave, 3rd Baron Segrave (-bef1326). Notable ancestors include Henry II of England (1133-1189), William I of England (1027-1087), Charlemagne (747-814), Hugh Capet (c940-996), Alfred the Great (849-899). Ancestors are from France, the United Kingdom, Germany, England, Belgium, the Netherlands, Italy, Russia, Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice FitzAlan was born in 1289, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel, was 22 and her mother, Countess Alesia di Saluzzo, was 20. She married Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave after 1316. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 7 February 1340, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Chacombe, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice FitzAlan est née en 1289, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Countess Alesia di Saluzzo, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave après 1316. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 7 février 1340, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Chacombe, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice FitzAlan 1289 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel 22 und ihre Mutter Countess Alesia di Saluzzo 20. Sie hat nach 1316 Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 7. Februar 1340 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Chacombe, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice FitzAlanが1289年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundelは22歳，母Countess Alesia di Saluzzoは20歳であった。1316年以後にStephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segraveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1340年2月7日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで51歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアチャコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice FitzAlan(이)가 1289년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Alesia di Saluzzo(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1316년 이후 Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1340년 2월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 차코브에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave após 1316. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice FitzAlan родилась в 1289 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Countess Alesia di Saluzzo, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen de Segrave 3rd Baron Segrave после 1316 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 7 февраля 1340 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности чакомб, нортамптоншир, Англия.
